options.content:
## CI-CD / Jenkins Controller — what this board shows
Metrics come from the **Jenkins OpenTelemetry plugin** (service `jenkins`). Two families:
- **`ci_pipeline_run_*`** — OpenTelemetry **CI/CD semantic-convention** metrics, one series **per pipeline** (`ci_pipeline_id`): launched / started / active / completed / **success / failed / aborted** + run **duration** histogram. These are emitted **as builds run** — if no build has run in the selected time range, the *Pipeline* panels are flat/empty (not a fault). Filter with **`ci_pipeline_id`**.
- **`jenkins_*`** — **controller internals**, continuously while Jenkins is up: **executors** (busy/idle/…), **build queue** (blocked/buildable/waiting), **agents/nodes** (online/offline), **plugins**, **SCM events**. These have data whenever the controller is running.

### What to look at
- 🟢 **Throughput & success** — build success rate %, completed-by-result, failed builds. Is the pipeline green?
- 🟡 **Latency & queue** — run duration p50/p95 per pipeline, queue size & **wait time** (jobs waiting for a free executor).
- 🔵 **Capacity** — executor utilisation (busy/total), agents online, SCM-event pool — is the controller a bottleneck?

> Pipeline ids here are the seed jobs (e.g. `gateway`, `jhipstersamplemicroservice`, `microservices-k6-smoke`). Trigger builds (or the seed job) to populate the pipeline panels. Controller (queue/executor/agent) panels populate as long as Jenkins is up. JVM of the controller is summarised here — full depth in **CI-CD JVM internals** (`service_name=jenkins`).

panels.title: Run duration p50 / p95 by pipeline
Avg queue wait time
panels.description: Mean time a job waited in the queue before an executor picked it up.

panels.title: SCM event pool — active threads vs queued
Controller JVM (summary — deep dive in 'CI-CD JVM internals')

targets.expr: {k8s_namespace_name="jenkins"} | detected_level=~"$level" | json | line_format "{{ if .message }}{{ .message }}{{ else if .msg }}{{ .msg }}{{ else }}{{ __line__ }}{{ end }}"

panels.title: Jenkins traces — pipeline / stage / step spans
panels.description: Build traces from the OTel plugin: pipeline → stage → step spans. Click a Trace ID → trace view (links to logs).
